title: swartzia panamensis
intro: Swartzia panamensis, also known as the Panamanian tree or Guarea panamensis, is a plant species in the family Leguminosae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and various skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Swartzia panamensis include its ability to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Swartzia panamensis can be made by infusing the leaves or bark in hot water to create teas, or by using the plant's extracts in topical creams and ointments.
